title: What's new for AI on GKE: Training, serving, and agents
summary: Google Cloud presented GKE innovations for AI workloads including training, serving, and agentic workflows. The platform has been updated to support reinforcement learning and complex agent-based systems with improved reliability, scalability, and cost-effectiveness. Companies like Thinking Machines Lab and Baseten are using GKE for production AI workloads.
writeup: Google Cloud Tech outlines recent GKE updates for AI workloads spanning training, serving, and agentic workflows. The platform now supports reinforcement learning and complex agent-based systems, with stated gains in reliability, scalability, and cost-effectiveness. Referenced concepts include model compression, local inference, and distributed training. Thinking Machines Lab and Baseten are cited as companies running production AI workloads on GKE.
